拒绝敏感型焦虑症(RSD)是一种极端的情感敏感和痛苦，由感觉到一个人在生活中被重要的人拒绝或批评而引发。它也可能是由一种失败感引发的-没有达到自己的高标准或别人的期望。
焦虑症是希腊语中“难以忍受”的意思。这并不是说患有注意力缺陷障碍(ADHD或ADD)的人是懦夫或软弱的人，而是情绪反应对他们的伤害比对没有这种疾病的人要大得多。没有人喜欢被拒绝、批评或失败。对于患有RSD的人来说，这些普遍的生活经历比神经典型的人要严重得多。它们令人无法忍受，令人受限，而且极具伤害性。
当这种情绪反应内在化时(对于患有RSD的人来说通常是这样)，它可以模仿一种完全的、主要的情绪障碍，并伴有自杀念头。由RSD引起的从感觉完全良好到极度悲伤的突然变化经常被误诊为快速循环情绪障碍。
医生可能需要很长时间才能认识到这些症状是由与ADHD和排斥敏感性相关的情绪突然变化引起的，而与他人相关的所有其他方面似乎都是典型的。事实上，RSD是一种常见的ADHD症状，特别是在成年人中。
当这种情绪反应被外化时，它看起来就像是对造成痛苦的人或情况产生的令人印象深刻的、瞬间的愤怒。事实上，在接受法院强制的愤怒管理治疗的人中，有50%的人以前没有认识到ADHD。
RSD可以让患有ADHD的成年人预料到被拒绝-即使是在任何事情都不确定的情况下。这会让他们对避免社交保持警惕，这可能会被误诊为社交恐惧症。社交恐惧症是一种强烈的预期恐惧，担心你会在公共场合出丑或羞辱自己，或者担心你会受到外界的严厉审视。
拒绝敏感度是很难分开的。通常，人们找不到词来描述它的痛苦。他们说这是激烈的，可怕的，可怕的，令人无法抗拒的。它总是由感知到的或真实的失去认可、爱或尊重而触发。
患有多动症的人主要通过两种方式来应对这头巨大的情感大象，这两种方式并不是相互排斥的。
1.他们变成了讨人喜欢的人。他们扫描他们遇到的每一个人，找出他们钦佩和赞扬的东西。然后他们把那个虚假的自我呈现给别人。通常，这会成为一个占主导地位的目标，以至于他们忘记了他们实际上想要从自己的生活中得到什么。他们忙于确保其他人不会对他们不满。
2.他们不再尝试。。如果有丝毫的可能性，一个人可能尝试新的东西，但在其他人面前失败或失败，那么做出努力就会变得太痛苦或太危险。这些聪明、有能力的人会避免任何令人焦虑的活动，最终会放弃约会、求职或在公共场合(无论是社交还是职业)发言等事情。
有些人利用RSD带来的痛苦来寻找适应和超越目标。他们不断努力，在自己的工作中做到最好，并努力追求理想化的完美。有时，他们被驱使成为无可非议的人。他们过着令人钦佩的生活，但代价是什么呢？
排斥敏感性是ADHD的一部分。这是神经学和遗传性的。童年早期的创伤会使情况变得更糟，但它不会导致RSD。通常，患者仅仅是知道这种感觉有一个名字就会感到安慰。知道这是什么，知道他们并不孤单，而且几乎100%的ADHD患者都会经历排斥敏感，这一点会有所不同。听到这个诊断后，他们松了一口气，知道这不是他们的错，他们没有受损。
心理治疗对RSD患者没有特别的帮助，因为情绪突然而完全压倒了大脑和感官。患有RSD的人需要一段时间才能在一段插曲后重新站稳脚跟。
最简单的解决办法是开一种阿尔法激动剂，如胍法辛或可乐定。这些药物最初是作为降压药设计的。胍法辛的最佳剂量从0.5毫克到7毫克不等，可乐定的最佳剂量从十分之一毫克到十分之五毫克不等。在这个剂量范围内，大约三分之一的人感到RSD缓解了。当这种情况发生时，改变的是生活。有时，这种治疗方法在治疗ADHD方面甚至比兴奋剂产生更大的影响，尽管兴奋剂对某些人也同样有效。
这两种药物似乎同样有效，但对不同的人群有效。如果第一种药物不起作用，就应该停止，然后另一种药物尝试。它们不应该同时使用，只能使用其中的一个。
第二种治疗是在标签外开单胺氧化酶抑制剂(MAOI)。传统上，这是经验丰富的临床医生治疗RSD的首选方法。它对ADHD的注意力/冲动部分和情感部分都非常有效。帕拉特(三环丙胺)通常效果最好，副作用最少。常见的副作用有低血压、烦躁、镇静和神志不清。
在20世纪60年代进行的一次面对面试验中，人们发现MAOIs对ADHD的疗效与哌醋甲酯一样有效。它们也几乎没有副作用，真正的一天一次剂量，不是受控物质(没有滥用的可能性)，有便宜的，高质量的仿制药版本，并且是FDA批准的用于情绪和焦虑症的。缺点是患者必须避免陈年而不是煮熟的食物，以及一线ADHD刺激性药物，所有抗抑郁药物，非处方药感冒，鼻窦和花粉热药物，非处方药止咳药。有些麻醉剂是不能用的。
支持问答感谢您阅读问答。为了支持我们提供ADHD教育和支持的使命，请考虑订阅。您的读者和支持使我们的内容和推广成为可能。谢谢。

Rejection sensitive dysphoria (RSD) is extreme emotional sensitivity and pain triggered by the perception that a person has been rejected or criticized by important people in their life. It may also be triggered by a sense of falling short—failing to meet their own high standards or others’ expectations.
 Dysphoria is Greek for “difficult to bear.” It’s not that people with attention deficit disorder ( ADHD or ADD) are wimps, or weak; it’s that the emotional response hurts them much more than it does people without the condition. No one likes to be rejected, criticized or fail. For people with RSD, these universal life experiences are much more severe than for neurotypical individuals. They are unbearable, restricting, and highly impairing.
 When this emotional response is internalized (and it often is for people with RSD), it can imitate a full, major mood disorder complete with suicidal ideation. The sudden change from feeling perfectly fine to feeling intensely sad that results from RSD is often misdiagnosed as rapid cycling mood disorder.
 It can take a long time for physicians to recognize that these symptoms are caused by the sudden emotional changes associated with ADHD and rejection sensitivity, while all other aspects of relating to others seem typical. RSD is, in fact, a common  ADHD symptom, particularly in adults.
 When this emotional response is externalized, it looks like an impressive, instantaneous rage at the person or situation responsible for causing the pain. In fact, 50% of people who are assigned court-mandated anger-management treatment have previously unrecognized ADHD.
  RSD can make  adults with ADHD anticipate rejection — even when it is anything but certain. This can make them vigilant about avoiding it, which can be misdiagnosed as social phobia. Social phobia is an intense anticipatory fear that you will embarrass or humiliate yourself in public, or that you will be scrutinized harshly by the outside world.
 Rejection sensitivity is hard to tease apart. Often, people can’t find the words to describe its pain. They say it’s intense, awful, terrible, overwhelming. It is always triggered by the perceived or real loss of approval, love, or respect.
 People with ADHD cope with this huge emotional elephant in two main ways, which are not mutually exclusive.
 1. They become people pleasers. They scan every person they meet to figure out what that person admires and praises. Then they present that false self to others. Often this becomes such a dominating goal that they forget what they actually wanted from their own lives. They are too busy making sure other people aren’t displeased with them.
  2. They stop trying. . If there is the slightest possibility that a person might try something new and fail or fall short in front of anyone else, it becomes too painful or too risky to make the effort. These bright, capable people avoid any activities that are anxiety-provoking and end up giving up things like dating, applying for jobs, or speaking up in public (both socially and professionally).
 Some people use the pain of RSD to find adaptations and overachieve. They constantly work to be the best at what they do and strive for idealized perfection. Sometimes they are driven to be above reproach. They lead admirable lives, but at what cost?
  Rejection sensitivity is part of ADHD. It’s neurologic and genetic. Early childhood trauma makes anything worse, but it does not cause RSD. Often, patients are comforted just to know there is a name for this feeling. It makes a difference knowing what it is, that they are not alone, and that almost 100% of people with ADHD experience rejection sensitivity. After hearing this diagnosis, they’re relieved to know it’s not their fault and that they are not damaged.
 Psychotherapy does not particularly help patients with RSD because the emotions hit suddenly and completely overwhelm the mind and senses. It takes a while for someone with RSD to get back on his feet after an episode.
  The simplest solution is to prescribe an alpha agonist like  guanfacine or clonidine. These were originally designed as blood pressure medications. The optimal dose varies from half a milligram up to seven milligrams for guanfacine, and from a tenth of a milligram to five tenths of a milligram for clonidine. Within that dosage range, about one in three people feel relief from RSD. When that happens, the change is life altering. Sometimes this treatment can make an even greater impact than a stimulant does to treat ADHD, although the stimulant can be just as effective for some people.
 These two medications seem to work equally well, but for different groups of people. If the first medication does not work, it should be stopped, and the other one tried. They should not be used at the same time, just one or the other.
 The second treatment is prescribing monoamine oxidase inhibitors (MAOI) off-label. This has traditionally been the treatment of choice for RSD among experienced clinicians. It can be dramatically effective for both the attention/impulsivity component of ADHD and the emotional component. Parnate (tranylcypromine) often works best, with the fewest side effects. Common side effects are low blood pressure, agitation, sedation, and confusion.
 MAOIs were found to be as effective for ADHD as methylphenidate in one head-to-head trial conducted in the 1960s. They also produce very few side effects with true once-a-day dosing, are not a controlled substance (no abuse potential), come in inexpensive, high-quality generic versions, and are FDA-approved for both mood and anxiety disorders. The disadvantage is that patients must avoid foods that are aged instead of cooked, as well as first-line ADHD stimulant medications, all antidepressant medications, OTC cold, sinus, and hay fever medications, OTC cough remedies. Some forms of anesthesia can’t be administered.

此外，这款应用程序提供了超过13种图标背景样式，每种样式有15种颜色，还有其他工具来构建定制体验，比如字形样式和徽章。总体而言，它有能力使用其内置工具生成13万亿个可能的图标-如果你选择在创建图标时使用自己的照片，那就更多了。
Launch Center Pro的开发者大卫·巴纳德(David Barnard)表示，为了实现这一点，许多工作去年已经在iOS13上完成。但iPhone主屏定制直到本月才真正开始，这要归功于iOS 14的推出。随着操作系统的更新，开发者终于可以在应用程序中附带不同大小的小工具，直接在用户的主屏幕上提供更吸引人的体验。
虽然最初的目的是将现有应用程序的信息更新带到主屏幕上，但一些开发人员利用新功能来构建专门的小部件设计工具。这些小工具制作应用程序允许用户使用各种颜色和样式创建多种类型和大小的小工具。例如，随着用户开始定制自己的主屏幕，Widgetsmith一直位居App Store排行榜榜首。
此外，一些用户想出了如何使用苹果的快捷方式来替换与他们最喜欢的应用程序相关的图标，以创造完全独特的、主题的主屏幕体验。TikTok上弹出了教程，#iOS14HomeScreen标签开始在Twitter上流行，人们分享他们iPhone改造的最终结果。
但重新设计主屏幕的一个障碍是，你要么需要找到一组自定义图标来使用，要么需要使用PicsArt或Photoshop等应用程序设计自己的图标。对于那些不经常使用创造性工具的人来说，这可能是一个挑战。这就是Launch Center Pro的用武之地：
@Launchcenterpro为iOS 14构建您自己的自定义图标！更多提示即将到来！##ios14 HomeScreen##ios14##HomeScreen♬原声-发布中心专业版。
这款应用程序提供了简单的工具，让你可以构建自己的图标，而不需要成为设计专家。相反，您只需选择图标形状、颜色和字形，然后可以选择添加框架或徽章。苹果的快捷方式应用程序提供了一套类似的工具，但选择要少得多。
然后，您制作的图标可以通过将图标导出到Camera Roll与快捷方式应用程序一起使用，也可以在Launch Center Pro的经典Today View小工具中使用。这些小工具不仅可以包括最喜欢的应用程序，还可以包括特定的操作或任务-比如给最喜欢的朋友发消息，获取方向或任何你通常在手机上做的事情。
花了大约一个小时使用@_davidsmith的优秀Widgetsmith和@LaunchCenterPro的图标创建了这个布局。#ios14homescreen pic.twitter.com/ZL6hBKY8MZ。
不幸的是，Launch Center Pro目前还没有发布兼容iOS14的主屏小工具。
然而，该团队预计将在今年秋天晚些时候准备好这些，以及其他重大更新。与此同时，该公司希望其图标设计器在iOS14定制的早期将派上用场。他们还计划在未来几周发布更小规模的更新，重点是改善图标设计体验。

In addition, the app offers over 13 icon background styles with 15 colors each, along with other tools to build a customized experience like glyph styling and badges, for example. In total, it has the capability of producing 13 trillion possible icons using its built-in tools — and even more if you choose to use your own photos when creating your icons.
   Much of the work to make this possible had already been done last year for iOS 13, says Launch Center Pro’s developer David Barnard. But iPhone home screen customization never really took off until this month, thanks to the launch of iOS 14. With the OS update, developers have finally been able to ship widgets of different sizes alongside their apps to offer a more engaging experience directly on users’ home screens.
 While the original intention was focused on bringing informational updates from existing apps to the home screen,  a handful of developers leveraged the new capabilities to build specialized widget design tools. These widget-making apps have allowed users to create widgets of many sorts and sizes, using a variety of colors and styles. Widgetsmith, for example, has been  topping the App Store charts as users began to customize their home screens.
 In addition, a number of users figured out  how to use Apple’s Shortcuts to replace the icons associated with their favorite apps in order to create entirely unique, themed home screen experiences. Tutorials popped up on TikTok and the hashtag  #iOS14homescreen began trending on Twitter as people shared the end results of their iPhone makeovers.
 But one obstacle to redesigning the home screen was that you either needed to find a set of custom icons to use or design your own using an app like PicsArt or Photoshop, for example. And this could be challenging for those who don’t regularly work with creative tools. That’s where Launch Center Pro comes in:
 @launchcenterproBuild your own custom icons for iOs 14! More tips to come!  ##ios14homescreen  ##ios14  ##homescreen ♬ original sound – Launch Center Pro
  The app offers simple tools that let you build your own icons without needing to be a design expert. Instead, you simply pick the icon shape, the color and the glyph, then optionally add a frame or badge. Apple’s Shortcuts app offers a similar set of tools, but with far fewer options.
 The icons you make can then either be used with the Shortcuts app by exporting the icon to your Camera Roll or they can be used inside Launch Center Pro’s classic Today View widgets. These widgets can include not just favorite apps, but specific actions or tasks — like messaging a favorite friend, getting directions or anything else you commonly do on your phone.
 Spent like an hour creating this layout using  @_DavidSmith’s excellent Widgetsmith and  @LaunchCenterPro for the icons.  #ios14homescreen  pic.twitter.com/ZL6hBKY8MZ
 Unfortunately, Launch Center Pro hasn’t yet released iOS 14-compatible home screen widgets at this time.
 However, the team expects to have those ready later this fall, along with other big updates. In the meantime, the company hopes its icon designer will come in handy in these early days of iOS 14 customizations. They also plan on releasing smaller updates focused on improving the icon design experience in the weeks ahead.
title: Launch Center Pro允许您构建自定义图标以自定义您的iOS 14主屏幕
title_en: Launch Center Pro lets you build custom icons to customize your iOS 14 home screen

YouTube正在加强措施，打击围绕即将到来的美国大选的错误信息。在今天的一篇博客文章中，该平台宣布了一系列新的变化，包括总统和国会候选人的搜索结果的变化，现在将在视频搜索结果之前有关于候选人的权威信息面板。
YouTube负责政府事务和公共政策的副总裁莱斯利·米勒(Leslie Miller)在宣布这些变化的帖子中表示：“随着11月3日的临近，我们正在努力使YouTube成为一个更可靠的新闻和信息来源，以及一个健康政治话语的开放平台。”
最大胆的努力是一项专门针对邮寄投票中的错误信息的新措施。从今天开始，处理邮寄投票的视频将在视频下面包含一个信息面板，引导观众访问来自两党政策中心的智囊团报告，标题为“邮寄投票计数”(Voting By Mail Counts)。该链接旨在消除有关邮寄投票导致欺诈的错误信息，类似于之前YouTube上关于登月、疫苗或其他常见阴谋论话题的信息小组。
邮寄投票阴谋具有政治敏感性，因为特朗普总统一直在鼓吹邮寄投票阴谋，特朗普一再毫无根据地声称，邮寄投票将导致欺诈。Twitter已经在几个场合给这些说法贴上了误导性的标签，但以它们具有新闻价值为由保持它们的可见性。
YouTube还将推出新的信息面板，用于查询选民登记，指向谷歌先前存在的“如何注册投票”和“如何投票”页面，这两个页面都是由民主工场(Democratic Works)等无党派合作伙伴填充的。
note_en:
YouTube is stepping up its measures against misinformation around the upcoming US election.  In a blog post today, the platform announced an array of new changes, including changes to the search results for presidential and congressional candidates, which will now have authoritative information panels about a candidate ahead of video search results.
 “As we approach November 3, we’re working hard to make YouTube a more reliable source for news and information, as well as an open platform for healthy political discourse,” said Leslie Miller, YouTube’s VP of government affairs and public policy, in a post announcing the changes.
 The boldest effort is a new measure specifically targeted at misinformation around mail-in voting. Starting today, videos dealing with voting by mail will include an info panel under the video directing viewers to a think tank report from the Bipartisan Policy Center,  titled “Voting by Mail Counts.” The link is meant to dispel misinformation about mail-in voting leading to fraud, similar to previous YouTube information panels on the Moon landing, vaccinations, or other common conspiracy theory topics.
 Mail-in voting conspiracies are politically sensitive because they’ve been so consistently promoted by President Trump, who has  repeatedly  and  groundlessly claimed that mail voting will result in fraud. Twitter has labeled the claims as misleading on  several  occasions but kept them visible on the grounds that they are newsworthy.
 YouTube will also roll out new information panels for queries on voter registration, directing to Google’s preexisting “how to register to vote” and “how to vote” pages, both populated by nonpartisan partners like Democracy Works.
title: 在美国大选之前，YouTube表示，它在关于邮寄投票的视频下加入了信息面板，将观众引向两党政策中心(Biartisan Policy Center)的一份报告
title_en: Ahead of US elections, YouTube says it is including info panels under videos about voting by mail, pointing viewers to a report by the Bipartisan Policy Center

note:
随着Moushik的启动，Shakti免费和开源硅项目达到了另一个里程碑，Moushik是一款与Arduino兼容的片上系统(SoC)，并且该集团第三次成功地进行了硅流片。
早在2018年，Shakti项目首次宣布成功地在基于自主研发的RISC-V处理器上启动Linux，最初是在美国半导体巨头英特尔(Intel)基于22 nm工艺制造的芯片上，然后在昌迪加尔的ISRO半导体实验室的180 nm节点上，在印度本地建造的芯片上启动。
现在，Shakti团队发布了第三款物理芯片：Moushik。“Moushik是一款处理器加系统的芯片，它将迎合快速增长的物联网物联网设备，这些设备是我们数字印度智能城市不可或缺的一部分，”该团队在谈到这款新设备时解释道。“制造微处理器芯片涉及三个步骤：设计、制造和硅后启动--所有这些步骤都是在印度完成的。
设计是在IIT Madras完成的，制造是在半导体实验室昌迪加尔进行的，主板印刷电路板设计是在IIT Madras再次完成的，这个主板是在Bengaluru组装和在IIT Madras进行硅启动后制造的。
同样基于180 nm工艺，Moushik在256针封装中拥有103个输入/输出(IO)引脚，由Shakti E-Class RISC-V内核供电。CPU在75 MHz至100 MHz之间运行，而SoC包括许多常用外设，包括SDRAM控制器、I²C、四路SPI、模数转换(ADC)、UART和用于调试的JTAG。
主板名为Adronics 1.0，包括对Arduino“盾牌”附加组件的支持，以加快嵌入式开发。“此外，主板还具有开关IC，可以实现多种电压之间的电力转换，”该团队补充道，“因此，各种外围设备都可以与主板连接。PCB是一块四层主板，输入功率为12伏，电流为2安培。“。
在一段视频中可以看到芯片经过了硅片后的启动过程，更多信息可以在Shakti的Twitter账户上获得。有关Shakti项目的更多信息，请查看AB Open的开源数字设计洞察力(OSDDI)系列中对Arjun Menon和Rahul Bodduna的采访。
note_en:
The SHAKTI free and open source silicon project has reached another milestone with the boot up of the Moushik, an Arduino-compatible system-on-chip (SoC) and the group’s third successful silicon tape-out.
 The SHAKTI project first announced its success in booting Linux on a home-grown RISC-V based processor  back in 2018, initially on a chip built by US semiconductor giant Intel on a 22nm process, then on a chip built natively in India on a 180nm node at the ISRO Semiconductor Laboratory in Chandigarh.
 Now, the SHAKTI team has announced its third physical chip: Moushik. “Moushik is a processor-cum-system on chip that would cater to the rapidly growing Internet of Things IOT devices that are integral part of smart cities of our digital India,” the team explains of the new device. “Three steps are involved in the making of a microprocessor chip: the design, the fabrication, and the post-silicon boot-up – all these steps were done in India.
 “The design was done in IIT Madras, the fabrication at the semiconductor laboratory Chandigarh, the motherboard printed circuit board design was done again at IIT Madras, manufacturing of this motherboard at Bengaluru assembly and post-silicon boot-up at IIT Madras.”
  Again built on a 180nm process, Moushik has 103 input/output (IO) pins across a 256-pin package and is powered by the SHAKTI E-Class RISC-V core. The CPU runs at between 75MHz and 100MHz, while the SoC includes a number of common peripherals including an SDRAM controller, I²C, quad-SPI, analogue-to-digital conversion (ADC), UART, and JTAG for debugging.
 The motherboard, dubbed Adronics 1.0, includes support for Arduino “shield” add-ons to speed embedded development. “In addition the motherboard also has switcher ICs which enable power conversion across a large variety of voltages,” the team adds, “thus enabling a variety of peripherals to be interfaceable with the motherboard. The PCB is a four layer motherboard with an input power of 12 volt at two amps.”
 The chip can be seen going through the post-silicon boot-up process  in a video, with more information available on the  SHAKTI Twitter account. For more information on the SHAKTI project in general, check out our interview with Arjun Menon and Rahul Bodduna in AB Open’s  Open Source Digital Design Insights (OSDDI) series.
title: Shakti宣布与Arduino兼容的Moushik获得第三个硅片成功
title_en: Shakti Announces Third Silicon Success with the Arduino-Compatible Moushik

note:
斯坦福监狱实验--有史以来最臭名昭著的心理学奇观之一--旨在展示环境是如何把我们所有人最坏的一面带出来的。在这项实验中，斯坦福大学教授菲利普·津巴多(Philip Zimbardo)指派付费志愿者在学校心理大楼地下室的模拟监狱里做囚犯或看守。这本应是一场自然主义的演习，讲述当一些人被赋予权力，而另一些人被剥夺权力时会发生什么。
关键的是，津巴多在1971年的一份文件中描述了这项实验，称“这些警卫没有得到如何成为警卫的具体指导或培训。”
“取而代之的是，”他说，“他们可以在一定范围内自由地做任何他们认为必要的事情，以维持监狱的法律和秩序，并赢得囚犯的尊重。”
本周，这个实验的故事发生了很大的变化。记者本·布鲁姆(Ben Blum)在一篇关于Medium的全面报道中发现了令人信服的证据，表明参与实验的警卫没有按照自己的愿望行事。录音和对参与者的采访显示，这些警卫被训练成刻薄的，或者被认为是一次“即兴表演”。这是其中一段录音，来自斯坦福档案馆。这是相当该死的。你可以听到津巴多的学生之一大卫·贾菲(David Jaffe)扮演监狱“典狱长”的角色，他在斥责一名警卫不够严厉。
(音频质量不是很好。毕竟，这是一种有近50年历史的盒式磁带录音。你可以阅读下面的文字记录。增加了重点。)。
杰菲：一般来说，你一直是幕后人物。部分原因是我的错，因为当他们在清点的时候，你想坐在外面的时候，我就顺从了。或者诸如此类的事情。但我们真的想让你活跃起来，参与进来。因为警卫必须知道，每个警卫都将成为我们所说的“强硬警卫”。到目前为止..。
杰菲：看，事情是这样的，我说的强硬是指你必须坚定，你必须在行动中，诸如此类的事情。这对实验的运作真的很重要，因为我们是否能让这个地方看起来像个监狱，这是事情的目的，很大程度上取决于警卫的行为。
这些发现极大地改变了实验的结论，如果你还可以这样称呼它的话。(许多人现在更多地认为这是一次戏剧性的演示，而不是一项严肃的科学努力。回想一下：它甚至在设定的结束日期之前就被终止了。)。一些警卫接受过训练，这一点消除了被置于权力位置会导致残忍的暗示。如果说有什么不同的话，那就是它表明了一个权威人物是如何说服另一个人顺从的。
纽约大学社会心理学家杰伊·范·巴维尔(Jay Van Bavel)根据这些发现在推特上写道：“底线是，从众不是自然的、盲目的或不可避免的。”“津巴多不仅在这一点上大错特错--他的公开评论还误导了数百万人，让他们接受了这个关于斯坦福监狱实验的虚假叙述。”
本·布鲁姆(Ben Blum)关于媒体的文章是一篇很棒的报道。请在这里阅读。
最近所有这些著名的心理学实验都在遭受抨击，这是怎么回事？我解释了监狱实验是如何适应该领域更大的“复制危机”的。
note_en:
The  Stanford Prison Experiment — one of the most infamous psychological spectacles of all time — was purported to show how circumstances can bring out the worst in all of us. In the experiment, Stanford professor Philip Zimbardo assigned paid volunteers to be either inmates or guards in a simulated prison in the basement of the school‘s psychology building. It was supposed to be a naturalistic exercise on what happens when some people are given power and others are denied it.
 Critically, Zimbardo  stated in a 1971 document describing the experiment that “the guards were given no specific instruction or training on how to be guards.”
  “Instead,” he said, “they were free, within limits, to do whatever they thought was necessary to maintain law and order in the prison and to command the respect of the prisoner.”
 This week, the story of the experiment changed considerably. In a  thoroughly reported exposé on Medium, journalist Ben Blum finds compelling evidence that the guards in this experiment were not left to act on their own desires. Audio recording and interviews with those involved reveal the guards were coached into being mean or considered the experiment to be an “improv exercise.” Here is one of those recordings,  via the Stanford archive. It’s pretty damning. You can hear David Jaffe, one of Zimbardo’s students who acted as the prison “warden,” chastising a guard for not being severe enough.
 (The quality of the audio is not great. It is, after all, a nearly 50-year-old tape cassette recording. You can read the transcript below. Emphasis added.)
  JAFFE: Generally, you’ve been kind of in the background. Part of that is my fault because I’ve gone along with when you wanted to sit outside while they were doing count. Or that sort of thing. But we really want to get you active and involved.  Because the guards have to know that every guard is going to be what we call a “tough guard.” And so far ...
    JAFFE: See, the thing is, what I mean by tough is you have to be firm, and you have to be in the action, and that sort of thing.  It’s really important for the workings of the experiment because whether or not we can make this thing seem like a prison, which is the aim of the thing depends largely on the guards’ behavior.
 These revelations significantly change the conclusions of the experiment, if you can even call it that anymore. (Many now think of it as much more of a dramatic demonstration than a serious scientific endeavor. And recall: It was even terminated before its set end date.) That some of the guards were coached cuts against the implication that being placed in a position of power leads to cruelty. If anything, it shows how an authority figure can persuade another into conformity.
 “The bottom line is that conformity isn’t natural, blind or inevitable,” NYU social psychologist Jay Van Bavel  tweeted in light of these revelations. “Zimbardo was not only deeply wrong about this — but his public comments misled millions of people into accepting this false narrative about the Stanford Prison Experiment.”
  Ben Blum’s piece on Medium is a great piece of reporting.  Read it here.
 What’s with all these famous psychology experiments coming under fire recently? I  explain how the prison experiment fits into the field’s larger “replication crisis.”
title: 斯坦福监狱的实验是建立在谎言基础上的。亲耳聆听(2018)
title_en: The Stanford Prison Experiment is based on lies. Hear them for yourself (2018)

note:
医生称，美国马萨诸塞州的一名建筑工人死于食用甘草的习惯。
这名男子没有透露姓名，但现年54岁，他每天大约吃一袋半的黑甘草。
在一家快餐店突然心脏骤停之前，他没有任何症状。
在“新英格兰医学杂志”上描述了这名男子的情况，他的医生说甘草中的甘草酸是罪魁祸首。
我们被告知这位病人饮食不佳，吃了很多糖果。埃拉泽·R·埃德尔曼医生说，他的病会不会与吃糖有关？
他说，研究表明甘草酸-甘草的有效成分-可能导致高血压、低钾血症、代谢性碱中毒、致命性心律失常和肾功能衰竭-所有这些都出现在这位患者身上。
这位病人最近还改变了他吃的糖果的类型。在他去世前几周，他从红色水果味的麻花换成了另一种用黑甘草制成的麻花。
另一位医生安德鲁·L·伦德奎斯特(Andrew L Lundquist)博士在报告中也同意甘草是罪魁祸首。
他写道：进一步调查发现，最近一种含甘草糖的变化可能是他低钾血症的原因。
note_en:
A construction worker in the US state of Massachusetts was killed by his liquorice habit, doctors say.
 The man, who has not been named but was 54 years old, ate about one-and-a-half bags of black liquorice every day.
 He had suffered no symptoms before suddenly going into cardiac arrest in a fast food restaurant.
 Describing the man's case in  the New England Journal of Medicine, his doctors said the glycyrrhizic acid in liquorice was to blame.
 "We are told that this patient has a poor diet and eats a lot of candy. Could his illness be related to candy consumption?" Dr Elazer R Edelman said.
  He said studies had shown glycyrrhizic acid - the active ingredient in liquorice - could cause "hypertension, hypokalemia, metabolic alkalosis, fatal arrhythmias, and renal failure" - all of which were seen in this patient.
   The patient had also recently changed the type of sweets he was eating. A few weeks before his death, he switched from red fruit-flavoured twists to another type made with black liquorice.
 Another doctor, Dr Andrew L Lundquist, agreed in the report that the liquorice was to blame.
 He wrote: "Further investigation revealed a recent change to a liquorice-containing candy as the likely cause of his hypokalemia."
title: 一名男子每天吃一袋以上的甘草致死
title_en: Man dies from eating more than a bag of liquorice a day

note:
两年前，氢气卡车初创公司尼古拉起诉特斯拉，声称特斯拉Semi是对尼古拉自己卡车设计的抄袭。周三，特斯拉在此案中提交了回应，指控尼古拉将自己的卡车基于设计师阿德里亚诺·穆德里(Adriano Mudri)2010年设计的一款概念车。穆德里现在是克罗地亚汽车制造商Rimac的设计总监。
阿德里亚诺·穆德里(Adriano Mudri)是Road Runner概念卡车的设计师，特斯拉在其法律文件中写道。路跑概念车进入了2010年米其林设计挑战赛，并被选为2010北美国际车展的展品。
根据特斯拉的说法，尼古拉创始人特雷弗·米尔顿在2014年和/或2015年与穆德里会面。特斯拉表示，米尔顿在申请尼古拉后来起诉特斯拉的设计专利时就知道Road Runner概念卡车，穆德里的设计对尼古拉的专利设计做出了重大的创造性贡献。
米尔顿和尼古拉设计师史蒂夫·詹尼斯被列为尼古拉专利申请的发明人，该专利申请没有承认穆德里对该设计的所谓影响。特斯拉辩称，这种不承认穆德里的做法应该使尼古拉的专利无效。
特斯拉还指出，尼古拉设计的几个元素-包括环绕式挡风玻璃和总体流线型形状-长期以来一直是其他半卡车的特色。特斯拉还包括了其他几辆卡车的照片，这些卡车的特征与尼古拉的专利设计相同。
尼古拉在给ARS的一份声明中表示，它将审查特斯拉的反诉，并做出适当回应。该公司补充说，尼古拉指控特斯拉的半卡车不仅侵犯了尼古拉的某些设计专利和实用专利，还侵犯了特斯拉的商业外观。在这份声明中，尼古拉表示，它将审查特斯拉的反诉，并做出适当回应。该公司补充说，尼古拉指控特斯拉的半卡车不仅侵犯了某些尼古拉的设计专利和实用专利，还侵犯了其商业外观。
尼古拉度过了艰难的一个月。两周前，卖空公司兴登堡研究公司(Hindenburg Research)透露，尼古拉的第一辆卡车尼古拉一号(Nikola One)从未开动过。尼古拉为这辆卡车制作了一段宣传视频，方法是将卡车滚上一座浅山，然后让它滚下来。米尔顿周日从该公司辞职，据报道，这场争议使尼古拉很难找到合作伙伴来帮助其建设计划中的加氢站网络。
note_en:
Two years ago, hydrogen-truck startup Nikola  sued Tesla claiming that the  Tesla Semi was a knockoff of Nikola's own truck design. On Wednesday, Tesla  filed a response in the case accusing Nikola of basing its own truck on a 2010 concept by designer Adriano Mudri. Mudri is now director of design at the Croatian automaker Rimac.
 "Adriano Mudri is the designer of the Road Runner concept truck," Tesla writes in its legal filing. "The Road Runner concept truck was entered into the 2010 Michelin Design Challenge, and was selected for display at the 2010 North American International Auto Show."
  According to Tesla, Nikola founder Trevor Milton met with Mudri "during 2014 and/or 2015." Tesla says that Milton knew about the Road Runner concept truck at the time he applied for the design patents Nikola later used to sue Tesla and that Mudri's design "constituted a significant inventive contribution" to the designs Nikola patented.
 Milton and Nikola designer Steve Jennes were listed as inventors on Nikola's patent applications, which failed to acknowledge Mudri's alleged influence over the design. Tesla argues that this failure to acknowledge Mudri should render Nikola's patents invalid.
 Tesla also points out that several elements of Nikola's design—including the wraparound windshield and general streamlined shape—have long been featured in other semi trucks. Tesla included photos of several other trucks that share features with Nikola's patented design.
 In a statement to Ars, Nikola said that it would "review Tesla’s counterclaims and respond appropriately." The company added that "Nikola alleges that Tesla's semi-truck infringes not only certain Nikola design patents and utility patents, but also its trade dress."
 Nikola has had a difficult month. Two weeks ago, the short-selling firm Hindenburg Research  revealed that Nikola never got its first truck, the Nikola One, working. Nikola created a promotional video for the truck by rolling it up a shallow hill and letting it  roll back down. Milton  resigned from the company on Sunday, and the controversy has reportedly  made it difficult for Nikola to find partners to help it build a planned network of hydrogen-fueling stations.
title: 尼古拉申请了一项被盗卡车设计的专利，特斯拉在法律回应中声称
title_en: Nikola patented a stolen truck design, Tesla claims in legal response

note:
对于注重隐私的人来说，互联网是一个充满敌意的地方。互联网提供商可以出售你的浏览历史记录，政府可以监视你，科技巨头可以收集大量数据来在网络上跟踪你。许多人求助于VPN或虚拟专用网络，认为它们可以保护你免受窥探者和间谍的伤害。
但在VPN试图解决问题的地方，它们也会让您面临更大的隐私风险。
TechCrunch的Romain Dillet解释了什么是VPN。简而言之，VPN最初是为员工在家中或出差时虚拟连接到其办公室网络而设计的。这些天来，
VPN更广泛地被用来隐藏你的在线互联网流量，并欺骗流媒体服务，让你认为你是另一个国家，而你并不是。同样的技术也可以帮助活动家和持不同政见者绕过他们自己国家的审查制度。
VPN的工作原理是将您的所有互联网流量通过加密管道传输到VPN服务器，使互联网上的任何人都更难看到您正在访问的网站或正在使用的应用程序。
但是VPN本身并不会保护你的隐私，也不会让你匿名。VPN只需将您的所有互联网流量从您的互联网提供商的系统转移到VPN提供商的系统即可。
这就回避了一个问题：为什么你应该相信承诺保护你隐私的VPN，而不是你的互联网提供商？答案是你不能，也不应该。
正如一句古老的谚语所说，如果它是免费的，那么你就是产品。这意味着他们会从你身上赚钱--具体地说，就是你的数据。就像任何免费的服务一样，VPN通常由广告提供支持。这意味着将你的互联网流量卖给出价最高的人，以便在你连接到VPN时为你提供定向广告。其他免费VPN被指控向你访问的网站投放广告。
虽然付费和付费VPN通常更关注您的隐私，但它们不是匿名的，因为它们可以链接到您的账单地址。付费VPN也不能解决将你所有的互联网流量输送到一个潜在的不值得信任的公司的问题。
一些VPN提供商还声称通过不存储任何日志或跟踪您访问的网站或时间来保护您的隐私。虽然在某些情况下这可能是真的，但你没有办法完全确定。
事实上，一些VPN提供商声称他们没有存储任何日志-但事实证明是完全错误的。
以UFO VPN为例，当时它有大约2000万用户。它声称有零伐木政策。但安全研究人员发现，该公司的日志数据库暴露在互联网上，不需要密码。数据库中堆满了用户活动的日志，包括用户访问的网站。
前纽约警察局网络情报和调查主任尼克·塞尔比现在是金融科技创业公司帕克索斯的首席安全官，他说他只使用他知道不存储任何日志的虚拟专用网提供商。他告诉TechCrunch，在他担任警察期间，他会发出搜查令，知道哪些供应商“最擅长什么都不给我”。
这并不是说所有的VPN都是肆无忌惮的，或者侵犯了你的隐私。VPN的问题很大程度上是，你不能查看引擎盖下的情况，看看你的数据是怎么回事。独立VPN(如Algo和WireGuard)允许您通过云服务(如Amazon Web Services、Microsoft Azure、Google Cloud或Digital Ocean)创建和控制您自己的VPN服务器。但请记住：您的加密数据存储在另一家公司的云中，因此很容易被当局窃取。
VPN可能很有用，但了解它们的局限性很重要。只是不要依赖他们来保护你的隐私或匿名。
note_en:
The internet is a hostile place for the privacy-minded. Internet providers can  sell your browsing history, governments can  spy on you, and tech titans  collect huge amounts of data to track you across the web. Many have turned to VPNs, or virtual private networks, thinking that they can protect you from snoopers and spies.
 But where VPNs try to solve a problem, they can also expose you to far greater privacy risks.
 TechCrunch’s Romain Dillet has an explainer on  what a VPN is. In short, VPNs were first designed for employees to virtually connect to their office network from home or while on a business trip. These days,
 VPNs are more widely used for hiding your online internet traffic, and tricking streaming services into thinking you’re another country when you’re not. That same technique also helps activists and dissidents bypass censorship systems in their own countries.
 VPNs work by funneling all of your internet traffic through an encrypted pipe to the VPN server, making it more difficult for anyone on the internet to see which sites you are visiting or which apps you are using.
 But VPNs don’t inherently protect your privacy or give you anonymity. VPNs simply divert all of your internet traffic from going to your internet provider’s systems into the VPN provider’s systems instead.
 That begs the question: Why should you trust a VPN that promises to protect your privacy more than your internet provider? The answer is that you can’t, and you shouldn’t.
   As the old adage goes, if it’s free then you are the product. What that means is that they make money off you — specifically, your data. Like any service that costs nothing, VPNs are often supported by ads. That means taking your internet traffic and selling it to the highest bidder to serve you targeted ads while you’re connected to the VPN. Other free VPNs  have been accused of injecting ads into the websites that you visit.
 While there are paid and premium VPNs that are generally more mindful about your privacy, they aren’t anonymous as they can be linked to your billing address. Paid VPNs also don’t solve the problem of funneling all of your internet traffic to a potentially untrustworthy company.
 Some VPN providers also claim to protect your privacy by not storing any logs or track which websites you visit or when. While that may be true in some cases, there’s no way you can be completely sure.
 In fact, some VPN providers have claimed they don’t store any logs — but were proven completely false.
 Take UFO VPN, which at the time had about 20 million users. It claimed to have a zero-logging policy. But security researchers  found the company’s logging database exposed to the internet, no password needed. The database was packed with logs of user activity, including which websites users were visiting.
 Former NYPD director of cyber intelligence and investigations Nick Selby, now the chief security officer at fintech startup Paxos, said he only uses VPN providers that he knows do not store any logs. During his time as a police officer he would serve search warrants and know which providers were “the best at giving me nothing,” he told TechCrunch.
 It’s not to say that all VPNs are unscrupulous or invading your privacy. Much of the problem with VPNs is that you can’t look under the hood and see what’s going on with your data. Standalone VPNs, like  Algo and  WireGuard, let you create and control your own VPN server through a cloud service, like Amazon Web Services, Microsoft Azure, Google Cloud, or Digital Ocean. But remember: your encrypted data is stored on another company’s cloud, making it potentially susceptible to being grabbed by the authorities.
 VPNs can be useful, but it’s important to know their limitations. Just don’t rely on them to protect your privacy or your anonymity.

出人意料的是，特斯拉(Tesla)在其最新专利诉讼中声称，尼古拉(Nikola)从Rimac的设计师那里窃取了其卡车设计。
正如我们之前报道的那样，尼古拉指控特斯拉的电动卡车设计侵犯了其现有专利。
具体地说，尼古拉因设计其尼古拉One卡车的几个功能而获得的一系列三项设计专利于2016年公布-一年后特斯拉推出了自己的电动卡车。
特斯拉否认这些说法，但自从尼古拉获得设计专利后，它现在要么可以证明自己没有侵犯专利，要么可以试图使专利无效。
在本周提交的一份新的回应中，特斯拉声称这些专利无效，因为尼古拉没有披露尼古拉One的一些功能是由阿德里亚诺·穆德里设计的，他最出名的是担任Rimac的设计总监。
阿德里亚诺·穆德里(Adriano Mudri)是Road Runner概念卡车的设计师。路人卡车是一款氢动力概念卡车。Road Runner概念卡车进入2010年米其林设计挑战赛，并被选为2010年北美国际汽车展的展品。下面转载了Road Runner概念卡车的几张图片。“。

阿德里亚诺·穆德里的公路跑步者概念卡车设计对设计专利中声称的发明做出了重大的创造性贡献。在起诉导致设计专利的申请期间，阿德里亚诺·穆德里没有被确认为发明家。“

note_en:
In a surprising turn of events, Tesla (TSLA) now claims Nikola (NKLA) stole its truck design from Rimac’s designer in an update to its patent lawsuit update.
  As we previously reported, Nikola alleged that Tesla’s electric truck design infringes on its existing patents.
 Specifically, a series of three design patents that Nikola obtained for the design of a few features of  its Nikola One truck unveiled in 2016 — a year before Tesla unveiled its own electric truck.
  Tesla denies the claims, but since Nikola obtained the design patent, it is now in a position to either show that it is not infringing on the patent or attempt to invalidate the patents.
 In a new response filed this week, Tesla claimed that the patents aren’t valid because Nikola failed to disclose that some of the Nikola One’s features were designed by Adriano Mudri, most known for being the Director of Design at Rimac.
  “Adriano Mudri is the designer of the Road Runner concept truck. The Road Runner truck is a hydrogen-powered concept truck. The Road Runner concept truck was entered into the 2010 Michelin Design Challenge, and was selected for display at the 2010 North American International Auto Show. Several images of the Road Runner concept truck are reproduced below.”
   Tesla claims that Trevor Milton met with Adriano Mudri “during 2014 and/or 2015” and he was aware of the Road Runner truck design, which has similar features as the Nikola One, including the wrap-around windshield — one of the designs that Nikola patented.
  “Adriano Mudri’s Road Runner concept truck design constituted a significant inventive contribution to the inventions claimed in the Design Patents. Adriano Mudri was not identified as an inventor during prosecution of the applications that led to the Design Patents.”
 Tesla argues that Milton had “deceptive intent” in not naming Mudri as an inventor in the patent application and that the patent office wouldn’t have issued the patent if they would have been aware that some features were based on a prior design by Mudri.
    When Nikola first filed the lawsuit, we  broke down each of the Nikola’s specific claims in what we believed basically amounted to a patent trolling case in a purely design-based objective way.
 The truth is that the patent office should have never granted the patents in the first place.
 They are all about vague design features that have been around for decades before Nikola ever existed and granting them only opens the door for some malicious people to do some patent trolling.
  I think the only winners in this situation, like it is often the case in those patent trolling cases, are going to be the lawyers involved.

这幅16世纪早期的油画可以说是达芬奇最著名的作品之一，目前存放在巴黎的卢浮宫。据估计，每年1020万游客中有80%的人会去看蒙娜丽莎。科学家帕斯卡尔·科特(Pascal Cotte)被要求使用专业相机将这幅画数字化，该相机能够捕捉肖像下面隐藏的层。从他的多光谱分析中，他发现了一些令人惊讶的细节，这些细节可能会打破之前对这项非凡工作的信念。
科特先生花了十年的时间分析了1650多幅图像，这些图像让人们对蒙娜丽莎以及达芬奇是如何创造它的有了复杂的洞察力。
他使用他的开创性的层放大方法(LAM)对一台能够探测13个波长的反射光的多光谱相机拍摄的图像进行处理，以捕捉光和物质之间的相互作用。
在这些层中，他发现使用了一种名为spolvero的技术，这种技术可以让这位意大利文艺复兴时期的画家使用木炭灰尘将素描转移到他的木制画布上。
这些标记表明这件作品并不完全是徒手画的，在达芬奇的其他作品中也发现了这些标记。
在蒙娜丽莎的下面，沿发际线和手部发现了斯波维罗印记，尽管这并不是他唯一发现的东西。
就在她额头的右边，他注意到一个似乎是发夹顶部的东西-一个小细节，提出了更多关于这项工作的问题。
科特先生告诉“每日快报”网站：“蒙娜丽莎头顶右边的这个发夹不可能属于一个人的肖像，因为在佛罗伦萨，这不是当时的时尚。”他说：“蒙娜·丽莎头像右边的这个发夹不可能属于一个人的肖像，因为在佛罗伦萨，这不是当时的时尚。”
“人们必须以特定的方式着装，以表示他们的职业和贵族对颜色的尊重。
“蒙娜丽莎不可能有这样的头发，当时在佛罗伦萨是不可能的。”
科特先生声称，这种类型的发夹更多的是用来形容“像女神一样虚幻的女人”，作为正义或善良的寓言，或者用在圣母玛利亚的画作中。
这枚发夹是用木炭做的徒手画的，其原因仍将是一个谜，但据推测，它可能完全是另一个项目的一部分。
科特怀疑，之前的两幅作品可能早于今天公众观察到的最后一幅《蒙娜丽莎》。
额头上的Sspolvero印记显示，达芬奇确实改变了这名女子头部和手的位置。

他告诉Express.co.uk：“他改变了头部的位置，让她直视你，就像一位母亲，每个人都有母亲，这样在看肖像的时候就可以分享和感受情感。”
斯波尔维罗画法是一种“非常快速的画像方式”，众所周知，达芬奇一生中的那个时候，他的画像需求量很大。
科特认为，这些发现“很可能会改变人们看待”蒙娜丽莎“的方式。”
他对路透社Express.co.uk表示：“这些发现增加了它的创造的神秘性，最终我们明白它是一个非常长的‘创造行为’的作品--它跨越了十多年，分了几个阶段。
“然后，公众的好奇心有时会如此强烈，以至于他们可能会想，去卢浮宫看她，是不是不可能猜到隐藏的肖像。”
帕斯卡先生声称已经发现了150件关于蒙娜丽莎的东西，他在2015年出版的“蒙娜丽莎的卢米埃：隐藏的肖像”一书中使用了他的LAM技术对此进行了详细描述。
他补充说：“我感到非常自豪，因为没有人付钱让我做这件事，我的发现要归功于我自己对科学的热情。”
“发现这些新元素并提出可靠的假说花了很长时间，我对此感到非常高兴。”

 The early 16th Century painting is arguably one of  da Vinci’s most famous works and currently resides in the Louvre, in  Paris. It’s estimated that 80 percent of their annual 10.2 million  visitors attend to see the Mona Lisa. Scientist Pascal Cotte was asked to digitise the painting using a specialist camera, which was able to capture hidden  layers beneath the portrait. From his multispectral analysis, he discovered a number of surprising details that could shatter previously held beliefs about the remarkable work.
    Mr Cotte spent a decade analysing more than 1,650 images, which give intricate insight into the Mona Lisa and how da Vinci was able to create it.
 He used his pioneering  Layer Amplification Method (LAM) on images taken by a multispectral camera that was able to detect light reflected on 13 wavelengths – to capture the interaction between light and matter.
 In the layers, he discovered a technique called spolvero had been used, which would have allowed the  Italian Renaissance painter to transfer a sketch to his wooden canvas using charcoal dust.
 The markings, which suggest the piece was not entirely freehand, had been discovered in other da Vinci works too.
 Beneath the Mona Lisa, spolvero marks were found along the hairline and the hand, although that is not the only thing he discovered.
 Just to the right of her forehead he noticed what appeared to be the top of a hairpin – a small detail that presents more questions about the work.
 Mr Cotte told  Express.co.uk: “This hairpin in the sky just to the right of Mona Lisa's head cannot belong to a portrait of a person because in the city of Florence this was not the fashion at the time.
   “People had to be dressed in certain ways to denote their profession and for nobility respecting the colours.
 “It is not possible for Mona Lisa to have hair like this, it was impossible of the time in the city of Florence.”
 Mr Cotte claims this type of hairpin is more typically used for an “unreal woman like a Goddess”, as an allegory for justice or goodness, or in a painting of the Virgin Mary.
 The reason for the hairpin, which was marked freehand in charcoal, will remain a mystery but it is speculated that it could have been part of another project entirely.
 Mr Cotte suspects two previous works could have preceded the final Mona Lisa observed by the public today.
 The spolvero marks along the forehead reveal that da Vinci did change the position of the woman’s head and her hand.
  To find out details of coronavirus in your area, please fill in your postcode below.
    He told  Express.co.uk: “He changed the position of the head to make her look right at you, like a mother and everybody has a mother so can share and feel emotion while looking at the portrait.”
 The  spolvero  technique is a “very fast way to make a portrait” and it’s known that  da  Vinci’s time was under great demand at that point in his life.
 Mr  Cotte  believes the discoveries “will probably change the way people look at the Mona Lisa”.
 He told  Express.co.uk: “These discoveries increase and increase the mystery of its creation, in the end we understand that it is the work of a very long ‘creative act’ – which spans more than a decade and in several stages.
 “And then, the  public's curiosity is sometimes so strong that they may wonder if it is not possible to guess the hidden portrait by going to see her at the Louvre.”
 Mr Pascal claims to have made 150 discoveries about the Mona Lisa, which he detailed in his 2015 book ‘ Lumiere on The Mona Lisa: Hidden Portraits’, using his LAM technique.
 He added: “I feel very proud because nobody paid me to do this, I made my discoveries thanks to my own passion for science.
 “To have discovered these new elements and to formulate a solid hypothesis took a long time and I am very happy with that.”

即将发布的三个型号分别是Eero 6路由器、Eero Pro 6路由器和Eero 6扩展器。Eero Pro 6是一个三频设备(一个2.4 GHz无线电，两个5 GHz无线电)；两个非Pro设备都是双频设备。这三个SKU都具有用于家庭自动化的内置Zigbee功能。

我们感到失望的是，亚马逊决定重新为路由器和扩展器单独设计设备，并明显倒退了两件式而不是三件式的基本套件。将游戏机或个人电脑等设备直接连接到卫星节点是整体Wi-Fi网络质量和可靠性的一大胜利，我们喜欢该公司早先做出的在所有产品上都配备以太网插孔的决定。我们还认为，绝大多数家庭最好是三件而不是两件。
亚马逊的Echo系列今天得到了更新，从早先平淡无奇的蹲式塑料圆柱体设计变成了更时髦的布面覆盖球体。全尺寸Echo第四代跑车售价100美元，将于10月22日推出木炭、冰川白或暮光之蓝三种款式。亚马逊告诉我们，要期待比前几代产品更好的音质，3；低音扬声器和双高音扬声器可以提供清晰的高音、动感的中音和自动适应任何房间的低音。
Echo Dot还进行了第四代改造，采用了新的球形设计，尺寸更小，有几个变种。基本款，有相同的木炭，冰川白，或暮光之蓝覆盖选项，成本是它的大兄弟姐妹的一半，大约50美元。还有售价60美元的Dot的高端版本，包括内置时钟的版本(仅限冰川白或暮光之蓝)，以及两个儿童版Dot-一个熊猫和一个老虎。
儿童版Dots还包括免费时间无限年-亚马逊为面向儿童的内容提供的精心策划的订阅服务-以及像儿童版Fire平板电脑那样的两年无忧更换保修。
最后是Echo Show 10，这是一个安装在电动万向节上的平板电脑，可以自动与检测到的人体对齐。人工智能的人体检测完全是机载的，而不是基于云的，它不寻找特定的人脸-相反，它会在房间周围寻找人形的轮廓。
我们设想售价250美元的Echo Show是制作蒙娜丽莎(Mona Lisa)印刷品的完美背景，蒙娜丽莎的眼睛真的会在房间里跟着你，但亚马逊提到了视频会议的一个更平淡无奇的用途。尽管这一功能可能很有用，但我们预计会有一些人习惯它--在新一轮的缩放失败视频中，与会者会忘记相机(仍然)在他们身上。Echo Show今天开始接受预订，但还没有具体的发货日期。
新的Fire TV Stick Lite售价30美元，非Lite Fire TV Stick售价40美元。这两种设备都包括Alexa语音远程功能和8 GB存储，处理器速度比以前的型号更快，最高分辨率为1080p。如果你想要完整的4K视频，你需要50美元的Fire TV Stick 4K，它今年没有收到任何更新。
今年的Lite和Non-Lite Fire电视棒的不同之处在于杜比音频(Lite上只有直通式HDMI，非Lite上有Full Atmos)和遥控器-Alexa Voice Remote只控制Lite上的棒本身，但它可以控制Non-Lite上的整个电视。
虽然新的Fire TV Stick和Stick Lite都只有1080p，但这两种设备都支持HDR(高动态范围)视频，这在4K设备和显示器上更常见，并且比非HDR视频提供更高的对比度和色域。
note_en:
Today at Amazon's hardware launch event, the company announced new Ring, Echo, Eero, and Fire devices. Amazon also announced a new  gaming service called Luna, which we're covering  in its own article.
   The Ring Always Home Cam is the newest device in the Ring family, which is better known for doorbells with cameras in them. The Always Home Cam is a tiny, self-docking drone designed to fly around  inside your home, streaming video off to the cloud for review in smartphone apps. Ring founder Jamie Siminoff says the Always Home Cam is intended to provide multiple interior viewpoints without the need for multiple interior cameras and that it's an "obvious product that's very hard to build."
 The drone operates fully autonomously, but the setup procedure involves mapping areas of the house in which it's allowed to fly and what paths it's allowed to take. After setup, the drone can be asked to fly directly, or it can fly on its own to visit disturbances detected by Ring alarm systems. Its 1080p camera is blocked by the dock itself, so if the drone is not in flight, it's not streaming or recording. Like most small drones, it integrates automatic obstacle avoidance and uses propeller shrouds to protect both the blades themselves and any objects, persons, or pets that might otherwise encounter them.
  The Ring Always Home Cam is expected to cost $250 and begin shipping some time in 2021.
   The Wi-Fi 6 Eero systems we  spotted FCC certifications for earlier this month were announced officially today. The new kits are available for preorder today, with shipping expected to begin in early November.
 The three models being released are Eero 6 Router, Eero Pro 6 Router, and Eero 6 Extender. The Eero Pro 6 is a tri-band device (one 2.4GHz radio, with two 5GHz radios); both non-Pro devices are dual-band. All three SKUs feature built-in Zigbee functionality for home automation.
 Both of the Router models feature two gigabit ports and one USB-C power port; the Extender has only the USB-C power port and does not offer wired connections. The Eero 6 Router can be preordered  solo for $130 or with one Eero 6 Extender  as a kit for $200. The Eero 6 Pro Router is available for  preorder at $230. It took a bit more digging, but we also found options to preorder a three-piece Eero 6  kit for $280 or a solo Eero 6  extender for $90.
 We're disappointed at Amazon's decision to go back to separate device designs for "routers" and "extenders"—and at the apparent step backward of making the base kit a two-piece, rather than a three-piece. Wiring devices such as game consoles or PCs directly to a satellite node is a big win for overall Wi-Fi network quality and reliability, and we liked the company's earlier decision to go with Ethernet jacks on all products. We also think the vast majority of homes are better served with three pieces rather than two.
     Amazon's Echo series got a refresh today, moving from the earlier, prosaic "squat plastic cylinder" designs to rather fancier cloth-covered spheres. The full-sized  Echo fourth-gen runs $100 and will be available October 22 in Charcoal, Glacier White, or Twilight Blue. Amazon tells us to expect better sound than previous generations delivered, with a 3" woofer and dual tweeters that deliver "clear highs, dynamic mids, and deep bass that automatically adapts to any room."
 The  Echo Dot also gets a fourth-gen makeover, sporting the new spherical design in a smaller size and in several variants. The base model, with the same Charcoal, Glacier White, or Twilight Blue covering option, is half the cost of its big  siblings at $50. There are also upscale variants of the Dot for $60, including versions  with a built-in clock (in Glacier White or Twilight Blue only), and two  Kids Edition Dots—one Panda and one Tiger.
 The Kids' Edition Dots also feature an included year of FreeTime Unlimited—Amazon's curated subscription service for all-you-can-consume kid-oriented content—and a two-year "worry-free" replacement warranty like the one offered on Kids' Edition Fire tablets.
 Finally, there's the Echo Show 10—a 10" tablet mounted on a motorized gimbal that automatically aligns itself to detected human bodies. The AI "human detection" is all onboard rather than cloud-based, and it does not look for specific human faces—instead, it looks for human-shaped outlines around the room.
 We envision the  $250 Echo Show as the perfect setting for a  Mona Lisa print whose eyes really  do follow you all around the room, but Amazon mentions a more prosaic use for video conferencing. As useful as this feature might be, we expect there will be some getting used to it—and a new round of "Zoom fail" videos in which conference participants forget the camera is (still) on them. The Echo Show is available for preorder today, but there is no concrete shipping date yet.
   A new  Fire TV Stick Lite is available for $30, and a non-Lite  Fire TV Stick for $40. Both devices include Alexa Voice Remote functionality and 8GB storage, with faster processors than previous models, and offer a max resolution of 1080p. If you want full 4K video, you'll need the $50  Fire TV Stick 4K, which has not received any updates this year.
 The difference between this year's Lite and non-Lite Fire TV Sticks lies in Dolby Audio (pass-through HDMI only on the Lite, full Atmos on the non-Lite) and remote control—Alexa Voice Remote only controls the stick itself on the Lite, but it can control the entire TV on the non-Lite.
 Although both the new Fire TV Stick and Stick Lite are 1080p only, both devices do support HDR (High-Dynamic Range) video, which is more frequently found on 4K devices and displays, and offers higher contrast and color gamut than non-HDR video.
title: 亚马逊新硬件：环形无人机、Echo Dot第四代、Wi-Fi 6 Eero等
title_en: New Amazon hardware: Ring drones, Echo Dot 4th Gen, Wi-Fi 6 Eero and more

Versa帮助Fitbit在经历了漫长而渐进的下滑后扭转了命运。该公司在接受智能手表方面行动迟缓，在推出Ionic时有点跌跌撞撞地走出了大门。但Versa找到了一个完美的甜蜜点，它建立在几代可穿戴健康技术的基础上，像Pebble这样的关键收购对象，以及200美元的定价甜蜜点。
事实上，苹果(Apple)和三星(Samsung)等其他大牌智能手表制造商后来也纷纷效仿，为这一类别提供了更划算的方式。这些举措出台之际，Fitbit和许多中国设备制造商正通过预算游戏抢占市场份额。不过，我想，在竞争激烈的同时，Fitbit也在走下坡路。这就是感觉的用武之地。这是对溢价定价的回归，该公司在让Ionic淡出时搁置了这一定价。
330美元的价格，感觉上属于溢价的低端-至少与Apple Watch Series 6和Galaxy Watch 3相比是这样，这两款产品的起价都是399美元。对于一家以较低价位取得成功的公司来说，高端市场并不是最合乎逻辑的选择，但或许可以理解的是，该公司有兴趣扩大自己的财富、遗留软件和健康指标，最终适当地试图在自己的游戏中击败苹果。
虽然Fitbit在市场上充斥着各种不同的设备，但我确实认为，在这里推出一款全新产品的决定是有意义的。事实上，Fitbit能做的最糟糕的事情就是给Versa增加一系列功能，并将价格提高100美元，从而在这个过程中消除了该产品的一个主要卖点(尽管Versa的价格也上涨到了220美元)。
事实是，感觉和逆序3实际上更相似，而不是不同。相似之处始于外壳。除了一些不同的颜色选择外，这两种产品看起来几乎是一样的。可以说，这种感觉来自于更时髦的颜色，要么是灰色，要么是金色。这款显示器是该公司熟悉的漩涡，只有一种尺寸可供选择。
我怀疑Fitbit从Ionic笨重的手表设计中吸取了教训。这款设备配备了1.58英寸的显示屏，感觉恰到好处。方形的设计让它感觉比苹果同等大小的手表更紧凑。也就是说，手表尺寸越大总是一件好事，特别是对于像Sense这样试图吸引广泛佩戴者的产品而言。
与三星(Samsung)和苹果(Apple)的智能手表不同，它没有独立的拨号键来在屏幕上导航。这款设备的侧面有一个压敏按钮，尽管这最终还是有点麻烦。我发现自己在移动手腕时反复不小心触发了它。老实说，对于大多数动作来说，简单地在屏幕上滑动是完全可以的。
顾名思义，Fitbit最新智能手表的最大区别在于传感器。这里的感觉很丰富。这两款手表都配备了光学心率监测器、体温监测器和SpO2传感器--这可能是Apple Watch Series 6宣布的最大一次升级。然而，Sense是唯一一款采用ECG传感器的Fitbit，在这方面与新的Apple Watch并驾齐驱。
就像这类传感器经常出现的情况一样，我无法在评论中真正强调这一点。FDA的审批似乎更简单一些，因为这一功能在消费设备上变得越来越普遍，但尽管Fitbit已经批准了这一功能，但这项功能要到下个月才能在设备上推出。当它真的到达时，你将得到我们期望从这些传感器获得的那种健康读数，包括心律和任何常见活动的通知。
睡眠跟踪是Fitbit一段时间以来一直击败苹果的一个领域。后者正试图用最新版本的watchOS来改变这一点，但为了迎头赶上，还有很多工作要做。传感器阵列对提供更完整的夜间睡眠情况大有裨益。虽然苹果的服务主要围绕上床时间和睡眠时间这样的内容，但Fitbit提供了更全面的情况，包括重要的睡眠质量，包括快速眼动(REM)、轻度睡眠和深度睡眠。脉搏氧饱和度和心率也被考虑在内。特别是SpO2，未来将成为睡眠中一个越来越重要的因素，因为这些设备希望跟踪睡眠呼吸暂停等情况。
睡眠的另一个重要因素是电池续航时间。这是Fitbit一段时间以来一直擅长的。感官评级为六天。然而，根据您是否选择了Always On Display和其他功能，您的里程会有很大的不同。目前的情况是，在关闭该功能的情况下，我一次充电可以充电几天。坦率地说，与苹果宣称的18小时相比，这是一个相当大的优势。每晚睡前或早上第一次充电并不理想。
我很欣赏Fitbit对正念的关注。我想这几天我们都可以多用一点。我绝对把我自己也包括在那艘船里。Fitbit是为数不多的几家智能手表制造商之一，目前正寻求将这一概念推向简单的呼吸练习之外。这包括在正念方块中。该公司将使用车载传感器对放松进行量化。老实说，我用得不多，但任何能帮助我开始正念练习的东西都是绝对积极的。
Sense的软件仍然是相当基础的。虽然有大量的手表面孔，但应用程序的选择落后于一些更大的品牌。如果谷歌收购成功，Fitbit在软件方面的做法将会发生怎样的变化，这将是一件有趣的事情。毕竟，wearOS已经存在了一段时间，并收到了大量更新，但仍有其不足之处。
Sense的强项也是Fitbit的强项：专注于健康和健身的强大基础。当然，该公司在升级方面有着良好而坚实的历史。但是，尽管它比Versa 3搭载了更多的传感器，但对许多人来说，差异相对较小-也许很难证明100美元的价格差距是合理的。我还没有花很多时间在这款设备的最新版本上，但如果过去有什么开场白的话，对于那些寻找价格合理、与Android兼容的苹果替代品的人来说，这是一个可靠的选择。
note_en:
The Versa helped Fitbit reverse its fortunes after a long, gradual slide. The company was slow to embrace the smartwatch, and stumbled somewhat out of the gate with the Ionic. But the Versa found a perfect sweet spot that build atop generations of wearable health knowhow, key acquisitions like Pebble and a pricing sweet spot at $200.
 In fact, other big-name smartwatch makers like Apple and Samsung have since followed suit, offering up more budget-friendly approaches to the category. The moves came as  Fitbit   and a slew of Chinese device makers were nabbing market share through budget plays. But while the competition zigs, Fitbit zags, I suppose. That’s where the Sense comes in. It’s a return to the premium pricing the company put on hold when it let the Ionic fade away.
  At $330, the Sense falls under the lower end of premium — at least compared to say, the Apple Watch Series 6 and Galaxy Watch 3, which both start at $399. The premium market isn’t the most logical play for a company that has been successful at a lower price point, but it’s perhaps understandable that the company is interested in expanding on its fortunes, legacy software and health metrics and finally, properly trying to beat Apple at its own game.
  While Fitbit has a bit of a history flooding the market with different devices, I do think the decision to introduce an entirely new product makes sense here. Really, the worst Fitbit could have done was add a bunch of features to the Versa and raise the price by $100, thus removing one of the product’s primary selling points in the process (though the Versa’s price has also increased to $220).
 The truth is, the Sense and Versa 3 are actually more alike than they are different. The similarities start with the casing. The two products look virtually identical, aside from some different color options. The Sense comes in, arguably, classier colors with either gray or gold. The display is the company’s familiar squircle, which is only available in the one size.
 I suspect Fitbit learned its lesson about unwieldy watch designs with the Ionic. The device sports a 1.58-inch display, which feels about right. And the square design makes it feel more compact than Apple’s comparably sized watch. That said, more watch sizes is always a good thing, particularly for a product like the Sense that’s attempting to appeal to a wide range of wearers.
 Unlike Samsung and Apple’s smartwatches, there’s no standalone dial-button for navigating through screens. There is a pressure-sensitive button on the side of the device, though that ultimately was a bit of a nuisance. I found myself repeatedly accidentally triggering it while moving my wrist. And honestly, for most actions, simply swiping across screens is perfectly fine.
  As the name implies, the biggest difference between Fitbit’s latest smartwatches comes down to sensors. The Sense has a lot packed in here. Both feature optical heart-rate monitoring, a temperature monitor and an SpO2 sensor — which was possibly the biggest upgrade announced for the Apple Watch Series 6. The Sense, however, is alone as the first Fitbit to adopt an ECG sensor, bringing it up to speed with the new Apple Watch on that front.
 As is often the case with these sorts of sensors, I’m unable to really highlight it in the review. FDA clearance seems to be a bit more straightforward as the feature has become more and more common on consumer devices, but while Fitbit has cleared it, the functionality won’t be rolled out on the devices until next month. When it does arrive, you’ll get the kind of health readouts we’ve come to expect from these sensors, including heart rhythms and notifications for any usual activity.
  Sleep tracking is one place Fitbit has had Apple beat for some time. The latter is attempting to change that with the latest version of watchOS, but still has a lot of work to do in order to catch up. The array of sensors go a long ways toward providing a more complete picture of your sleep over the course of the night. While Apple’s offering largely revolves around things like time in bed and time asleep, Fitbit provides a fuller picture, including, importantly, sleep quality, broken up by REM, light and deep sleep. SpO2 and heart rate are also factored into the picture. SpO2, in particular, will become an increasingly important factor in sleep going forward, as these devices look to track things like sleep apnea.
 Another big piece of sleep is battery life. That’s something Fitbit’s been good at for a while. The Sense is rated at six days. Your mileage is going to vary considerably, however, depending on whether you opt for the always on display and other features. As it stands, I was able to get several days on a single charge with the feature switched off. Frankly, that’s a pretty big advantage over Apple’s stated 18 hours. Charging each night before bed or first time in the morning isn’t ideal.
  I appreciate Fitbit’s focus on mindfulness. I think it’s something we can all use a bit more of these days. I definitely include myself in that boat. Fitbit is one of a handful of smartwatch makers currently looking to push the concept beyond simple breathing exercises. That’s included in the Mindfulness tile. The company will quantify relaxation using the the on-board sensors. I honestly haven’t used it a ton, but anything that can help jumpstart a mindfulness practice is a net positive.
 The Sense’s software continues to still be fairly basic. And while there are plenty of watch faces, the app selection lags behind some of the bigger names. It will be interesting to watch how Fitbit’s approach to software changes if/when the Google acquisition goes through. After all, wearOS has been around for a while and received plenty of updates, but still has its share of shortcomings.
  The Sense’s strong suit is also Fitbit’s: a strong underpinning of health and fitness focus. The company certainly has a good, solid history of upgrades. But while it packs more sensors than the Versa 3, for many the difference will be relatively minor — and perhaps difficult to justify that $100 price gap. I’ve yet to spend a lot of time with the latest version of that device, but if past is any prologue, it’s a solid choice for those looking for an Android-compatible Apple alternative at a good price.

尽管创造者经济十年前才诞生，但世界上有超过5000万人认为自己是创造者。它已经成为成长最快的小企业类型，一项调查发现，更多的美国孩子长大后想成为YouTube明星(29%)，而不是宇航员(11%)。
我们已经为100多家顶级初创公司和工具创建了速成课程，旨在帮助有影响力的人，因此，无论您是寻求帮助的创建者、寻找机会的创始人，还是寻找下一艘火箭的投资者，这张市场地图都将让您对创建者生态系统有一个广泛而深入的了解。我保证接下来10分钟的阅读不会被广告打断。不需要高级会员资格。
创作者经济的定义，也称为激情经济，是指以独立内容创作者、策展人和社区建设者(包括社交媒体影响力者、博客作者和录像师)为中心的一类企业，外加旨在帮助他们实现增长和盈利的软件和金融工具。
使用YouTube、Instagram、Snapchat、Twitch、TikTok、Substack、Patreon和OnlyFans等平台，内容创作者可以通过以下方式赚钱：
如果你是这个领域的创始人，SignalFire会很乐意收到你的来信！我们资助了一些工具来帮助有影响力的人运营和盈利，比如信用卡Karat，并领导了YouTube联合创始人查德·赫尔利的新公司Greenpark的种子融资。我们为早期和中期的初创企业提供资金，并在招聘、咨询、上市战略和公关方面为它们提供帮助。所以，不要羞于进入我们的DM或点击下面的时事通讯订阅按钮。
那么，创作力是如何发展得如此之快的呢？社会意识发生了转变，更多地关注工作中的成就感，控制我们如何度过时间，成为我们自己的老板。粉丝们看到创作者们以做他们喜欢的事情为生，并渴望走上那条永远不会通向小隔间的道路。
与此同时，手机上更好的摄像头，更大的屏幕，更快的移动网络，以及以创作者为中心的社交网络，都刺激了该行业的拐点。现在，加入创作者俱乐部所需要的只是一部电话，一个想法，以及愿意接受陌生人评判的意愿。简单？不完全是。这将保证你有12次观看(如果你有很多表亲，也许是15次)。做梦也别想着品牌交易。要想成功，创作者必须是令人难以置信的故事家、无情的推销者和粉丝社区的领导者。
幸运的是，已经建立了大量的公司来支持创作者，特别是能够将其作为全职职业的200万人(想象一下，如果立陶宛的所有人口都成为YouTubers，需要多少工具和基础设施)。
以下是我们自下而上的TAM(总可寻址市场)分析，总共有5000万创作者：
Instagram：在Instagram上的10亿个账户中，约50万个账户拥有超过10万粉丝，被认为是积极的影响力者(来源)。
Twitch：在Twitch上的3M流媒体中，约300K具有合作伙伴或合作伙伴身份(来源)。
在我们深入研究所有类型的工具之前，了解创造者经济的演变是很重要的，创造者经济可以分为三个不同的层次，它们相互建立在一起。
第1层：基础媒体平台。自本世纪头十年末以来，我们见证了YouTube、Instagram、iTunes、Spotify等平台的诞生，最近又出现了Snapchat、Twitter、Medium、Twitch、TikTok等平台。这些平台通过在推荐和管理算法上投入大量资金，帮助创作者被发现并建立受众-它们解决了创作者的分发问题。创作者不再受大型制作公司的摆布，这些公司决定制作什么内容和观众是谁。这些平台促成了Maker和FullScreen等多频道网络的崛起。他们聚集了创作者，并在他们被以数亿美元收购之前为他们配备了受众发展工具，同时出现了像Brat TV和Tastemade这样的新网络。这些平台还需要创建多媒体编辑工具，帮助创作者完善他们的内容。但平台并不总是把内容贡献者的最佳利益放在心上，所以聪明的创作者学会了在不同的应用程序上交叉推广和多样化他们的存在，以最大限度地减少“平台风险”。这样一来，他们就不会因为某个平台的衰落、优先级的改变、功能的移除或机会的减少而受到伤害，这就是所谓的“平台鞭打”(Platform Whiplash)。
第2层：将影响者范围货币化。一旦顶级创作者建立了信任他们必须说的话的既定受众，品牌就开始认识到付费创作者利用他们在平台上的触角为产品和服务做广告的投资回报。虽然一些平台与创作者分享传统的广告收入，但另一些平台则让内容制造商想办法赚钱，导致赞助内容和撮合交易的利基等公司的崛起。现在这个领域有数百家公司，包括有影响力的机构、赞助市场、人才代理公司等等。根据Mediakix的数据，目前有影响力的TAM营销约为80亿美元，预计到2022年将增长到150亿美元，使其成为增长最快的商业领域之一。理想情况下，创作者与与其个人品牌相匹配的赞助商合作，而不是牺牲内容质量来公开宣传公司信息。然而，随着影响力营销变得越来越普遍，越来越多的品牌开始为其付费，有影响力的人注意到了一个模式：每发表一篇付费帖子，他们就会失去与受众建立的一些信任，从而损害他们的参与度和增长。这就把我们带到了最新一波创作者进化…。
第三层：创建者即企业。这就是我们今天所处的位置！在开发了平台外的追随他们的狂热分子后，创作者可以成为成熟的企业，除了广告之外，还有多种收入来源。公司已经来到这里，通过销售优质内容、商品、书籍/电子书、时事通讯等产品或销售粉丝参与、教练、咨询、演讲约定等服务来帮助创作者赚钱。这让他们专注于取悦最大的粉丝，制作更独特的利基内容，而不是拼命寻找尽可能多的受众，制作更通用的点击内容。
本质上，创作者必须平衡某些平台的分销潜力和变得依赖它们的风险，并通过以下方式赚钱：要么从主流内容中为大量观众从每个粉丝身上赚取一点收入，要么通过利基内容从与较少粉丝的更深层次联系中赚取大量收入。
我们在这里看到的大趋势是，随着时间的推移，创作者的收入来源变得更加多样化，并直接由粉丝提供资金。
创作者已经从YouTube等平台向他们支付广告收入份额，以换取将观众带到这些平台上，变成由Instagram和Snapchat上的品牌赞助商支付，以换取他们通过这些平台接触到的受众，再到由粉丝通过赞助、小费或电子商务支付，以换取平台以外的娱乐和社区。
现在我们已经讲完了简短的历史课，让我们来谈谈具体的子行业和其中令人兴奋的公司吧！我们还将讨论新冠肺炎的影响，以及我们对每个公司的投资机会的评估。
新冠肺炎有什么影响？随着消费者对娱乐的参与度增加，出现了很大的顺风。随着用户在这些应用上花费更多的时间，他们会关注更多的创作者，消费更多的内容，并为他们赚取更多的钱。反过来，这可以增加平台的收入，同时让创建者更愿意为帮助他们的工具付费。
优点：一旦这些平台获得了吸引力，它们就可以通过病毒迅速增长。在这个领域已经取得了巨大的成果。
缺点：非常饱和的市场，每个类别的赢家都已经确定了。对于新进入者来说很困难，因为现有的都有很强的网络效应。
一些平台有自己的嵌入式内容创建工具(如TikTok视频特效和Instagram照片滤镜)，但也有许多公司提供点式解决方案来制作增强内容。从历史上看，附加了社交网络的内容创作工具在财务上是最成功的。
一个有意义的例外是这个领域的巨头Adobe Creative Cloud，它包括Photoshop和其他几个著名的工具，如Premier Pro和Illustrator。2013年，Adobe将其商业模式从销售单个软件许可证(全套软件的价格为1300-2600美元)转变为销售订阅(全套软件的价格为每月52美元)。然而，Adobe的大多数客户是商业创建者(即在某些公司的营销团队工作的人)，而不是在社交平台上发布的个人创建者。
一些平台进行了收购，成为发现、创造和货币化的“一体机”目的地。2017年，Spotify收购了音乐制作软件开发商SoundTrap，因此它可以提供制作音乐的方法，而不仅仅是发行。
今天，创作者拼凑多个工具来编辑他们的内容并从中赚钱是很常见的，然后他们在社交网络上分享他们的内容。例如，Instagram的创建者可能会资助使用Karat拍摄，在Snapchat中录制，使用invideo或Pixlr编辑，然后发布到Instagram，在那里他们使用GRIN或Captiv8在平台上赚钱，使用Teespring和Cameo在平台外赚钱，使用Delmondo跟踪他们的分析。
视频：Streamlabs的OBS(Open Broadcast Software)是一款用于录制直播视频的开源应用程序，invideo和PlayPlay等工具都是基于移动和网络的视频编辑工具，可以帮助创作者将视频格式化为Instagram、Facebook等上的社交共享格式。Vochi是一款基于应用程序的视频编辑器，可以更上一层楼，制作非常酷的视频效果。其他工具，如Kapwing，采取更水平的方式，帮助编辑视频、音频，甚至GIF。像Trash和Quik这样的应用程序会自动将短视频剪辑与音乐编辑在一起，以制作可以在垃圾桶或任何社交网络上分享的迷你电影。创作者甚至在Peerspace和Breather上按小时出租房间，这样他们就有了拍摄视频的地方。
摄影/平面设计：亲和力是一家总部位于英国的公司，一直在挑战Adobe的霸主地位。每个完整的访问许可证只需50美元，Affinity为创建者提供了更实惠的专业照片、设计和布局编辑器。其他基于网络的低成本照片编辑器包括Pixlr、picMonkey和Fotor。像Canva和Snappa这样的公司更专注于成为各种媒体的平面设计编辑-传单、名片、宣传册、邀请函、照片等。还有一些公司像PiktoChart是为信息图表设计而生的。当然，美图和FaceTune可以让用户修平瑕疵，美白牙齿，甚至改变脸型，让他们看起来更有吸引力。
Canva最近以60亿美元的估值登上了头条，可以说是近年来最有价值的内容创作公司之一。令人惊讶的是，Canva的所有收入都来自个人升级为高级会员，这让用户可以即时访问60K的模板(而免费层只有8K)和一个包含6000万库存照片、GIF、视频和图形等的库。Canva之所以成为一个比其他所有工具都好10倍的工具，是因为他们广泛的设计构建块宝库，使得创造美丽的东西变得容易。
动态照片：除了两个最大的GIF创作平台Giphy和Gfycat之外，一种新型的媒体格式--“视频迷因”--正在兴起。看看Pinata，它应该很快就会推出公测版。
音乐：这个领域真的没有那么多初创公司，可能是因为它的市场比其他一些媒体格式小得多。许多艺术家使用GarageBand，它拥有创作音乐的大部分基本功能。专业人士可能会从FL Studio和Ableton Live等几家销售可下载音乐制作软件的大型传统公司中的一家购买许可证，或者求助于Splice进行音乐制作协作，以及买卖歌曲中使用的音频样本。与此同时，通过流媒体平台进行发行，通过社交网络进行营销，通过赞助平台实现货币化，CD等实体格式的消亡，消除了对传统唱片公司的许多需求。
播客：作为播客格式的热门领域，近年来变得更加流行。SignalFire的投资组合公司之一RedCircle帮助播客接触到新的受众，并通过将他们与品牌联系起来获得广告机会，将他们的内容货币化。其他帮助发布播客的公司包括PodBean、麦克风、Buzzsprout和Anchor。
COVID撞击？随着越来越多的人呆在家里，要么成为创作者以获得额外的收入来源，要么现有的创作者有更多的时间投入到他们的创作中，这带来了很大的顺风。
缺点：Adobe是这一领域的巨头，具有明显的网络效应(它已经成为允许创建者之间共享的文件类型标准)。创作者的单点解决方案很难货币化，除非它有明确的ROI，因为创作者/创作者的产品非常差异化和具有防御性。
专业的影响力营销机构。各品牌聘请这些机构来识别高绩效的影响者，以接触特定的受众群体，协商影响者合同，与影响者保持一致的沟通，发起多个跨渠道活动，并进行深入的活动后分析和ROI评估。最大的有影响力的机构包括Mediakix、Pulse Advertising、Who Say和Everywhere。品牌之所以聘用他们，是因为他们与有影响力的人才或与他们合作的内部团队没有直接联系。但由于这些机构极其亲力亲为和个性化，对品牌和有影响力的人来说都非常昂贵。爱德曼(Edelman)和韦伯·尚德威克(Weber Shandwick)等公关巨头也加入进来，充当创作者和品牌之间的中间人，同时确保他们的客户不会受到玷污。
有影响力的营销平台和市场。对于品牌来说，这是一个更便宜的解决方案，可以访问他们可以从中过滤的影响者的大型数据库。这些平台通常收取软件即服务费用，外加每次活动的提成费率。虽然这些平台可能有内置的分析和报告功能，但缺点是，品牌通常无法接触到最具影响力的人，因为这些人通常只与机构合作。供应主要集中在中小有影响力的人身上，这些人的影响范围可能不会那么广，因此在营销支出上的投资回报率(ROI)也不会那么高。一些有影响力的营销平台包括GRIN、Captiv8、AspirreIQ、TapInfluence、Klear、The Shelf、CreatorIQ和Arhouse(以前是利基，归Twitter所有)，在这些平台上，品牌可以为他们的活动设定预算，并搜索符合他们标准的影响力者的大型数据库。传统的好莱坞经纪公司，如CAA，UTA和WME，经常与有影响力的营销巨头合作，让品牌接触到他们的主流名人。
像Plug这样的平台采取了不同的方式，让有影响力的人有权选择他们想要参与的活动。然而，他们平台上的广告商只根据业绩付费(即如果有人下载广告商的应用程序)。与传统的品牌活动不同，在这种情况下，有影响力的人的报酬直接与他们为品牌产生的ROI成正比。在这个更加量化的有影响力的营销和分析领域，其他参与者包括集合体和The Amplify(隶属于You&Amp；Mr Jones)、德尔蒙多(隶属于Conviva)和惠勒(Whalar)。粉丝和品牌都在关注著名生日的排行榜，这是一个有影响力的维基百科，寻找可以追随或合作的明星。一些有影响力的人销售创意服务来概念化和制作内容，而不必向他们的追随者发送垃圾邮件，一些人专注于向其庞大的受众销售现有内容分发的低成本业务，还有一些人两者兼而有之。
CRM工具。由于有影响力的人注册了多个有影响力的营销平台，并且通常有几个品牌合同同时进行，因此专门为有影响力的人(如Tubull Labs、MoeAssist和CloutJam)构建的CRM工具最近如雨后春笋般涌现出来，以帮助有影响力的人管理他们的工作流。
COVID撞击？由于企业和品牌不能亲自进行专业拍摄，因此他们正在接触创作者，以获取用户生成的内容，但其他品牌已经在很大程度上暂停了营销努力，以节省现金。
优点：鉴于品牌营销仍然是创作者创收的头号途径，我们有机会在平台/市场领域建立可扩展的大型业务。谁将成为有影响力的营销的双击手
缺点：这一领域还没有取得重大成果，Twitter收购Niche和谷歌收购FameBit的规模只有数千万美元。我们的假设是有几个原因：
这些平台都不能垄断供应-影响者受到激励，与尽可能多的平台签约。防御性是一个关键问题。
最具影响力的人与机构合作，而不是与平台/市场合作。这些平台通常捕捉到一长串中小型有影响力的人。
有影响力的人都有不同的个性，因此很难管理-平台没有准备好这样做。
由于业余有影响力的人有5000万的长尾，而且增长迅速，有影响力的人之间对品牌交易的竞争要激烈得多，这迫使他们寻找其他创收方式。
我们从探索各种捐赠平台开始，这些平台允许粉丝向他们最喜欢的创作者捐赠。这有几种不同的方式：
基于特别项目的资助。我们对Kickstarter、Indiegogo和GoFundMe等大型众筹平台都相当熟悉。创作者使用这些平台发布他们的一次性书籍、漫画、纪录片、短片、专辑等。这些平台通常收取5%的提成。
以订阅为基础的资金。“每月向你最喜欢的创作者订阅10美元”的概念是由杰克·孔蒂(Jack Conte)和他的初创公司Patreon基于对他的乐队Pomplamoose和他们的音乐视频制作工作室持续不断的财政支持的需要而创立的。最近，像Twitch和YouTube频道会员这样的媒体平台已经开始建设基础设施，让他们的创建者直接向他们的频道收取订阅费。但是，如果平台不断试图向粉丝交叉推广其他内容制作者，可能会增加第一个创作者的订户流失，这可能会让一些创作者感到恼火。
小费罐子概念。你也可以一次性捐款，而不是为你最喜欢的创作者设置循环捐款，这对粉丝来说参与的摩擦要小得多。像Ko-fi和Buy Me A Coffee这样的平台给了创作者一个平台，让他们可以在这里和那里向粉丝索要5美元。这些平台可以通过不要求预付费让创作者接触到他们的最大受众，同时仍然为粉丝提供一种自愿在经济上支持创作者的方式。一些较大的社交网络。
.
